greet.text: Deane is a hundred and thirty-five, and pays a Jersey clinic to keep him that way. He folds soft pink hands on the desk. "Sit. You have the look of a man who lost something and is hoping I found it. I usually have."

word.text: "Word is cheap, and I am not." The porcelain smile widens. "But for you — someone has been quietly buying up old cowboys' debts. If I owed anyone money, I believe I'd be watching the street behind me."

work.text: "There is always work for a man who can think in eight dimensions. Get your deck running, learn to break a little ICE, then come back and we'll talk numbers over ginger tea."

bye.text: "Do give my regards to Ratz. And try not to die before you're useful to me." He returns to a ledger no one else will ever read.
